text: We should be focusing on building the things that don't exist.
context: The anti-incremental spirit: invent the future instead of copying competitors.

text: The perfect search engine would understand exactly what you mean and give back exactly what you want.
source: Early Google vision / IPO letter era
context: The ambitious north star for organizing and democratizing all human knowledge.

text: Solving big problems is easier than solving little problems.
context: Counterintuitive idea that grand challenges attract better talent and resources.

text: We simply do not know what the limit to intelligence is.
source: Comments on AI and future potential
context: Open-ended optimism about the upper bounds of what technology can achieve.

text: Don't be evil. Do the right thing. Build products that make the world better.
speech: Don't Be Evil / Do the Right Thing
context: The famous guiding principle for technology's impact on humanity.

text: We want Google to be the third half of your brain.
context: Vision of seamless integration of information into human thought.
Insight on the need for freedom and risk-taking to drive breakthroughs.

text: We believed we could build a better search. We had a simple idea that not all pages are created equal.
context: The power of a clear, contrarian insight to change an industry.

text: We do lots of stuff. The only way you are going to have success is to have lots of failures.
context: Insight on embracing failure as essential to meaningful progress.

text: It's not enough not to be evil. We also actively try to be good.
speech: Don't Be Evil / Do the Right Thing
context: Evolution of the motto into proactive positive impact.
